rows.targeted_area: 32-bit zisofs block-pointer allocation in the ISO9660 reader
required_controls: use checked multiplication and addition when sizing zisofs block-pointer tables
reject images whose derived allocation exceeds the target architecture's addressable size
test 32-bit builds explicitly for size and count overflow behavior

rows.targeted_area: zisofs decompression in the ISO9660 reader
required_controls: validate `pz_log2_bs` against the format's legal range
use checked shift and size computations for block sizing
cap allocation requests derived from archive metadata

rows.targeted_area: RAR LZSS window handling across compression-method transitions
required_controls: revalidate LZSS window size after every compression-method transition
bind filter and copy operations to the active window bounds before decoding
treat inconsistent decoder state transitions as hard parse failures
detect iterations that consume no input and produce no output
bound decompressor work relative to consumed bytes
treat checksum-valid archives as still untrusted for progress guarantees
reject WARC content lengths that exceed representable internal limits
use checked arithmetic when adding framing overhead to claimed body sizes
cap read, skip, and allocation paths derived from WARC length metadata
check filter-block size against the currently active LZSS window before use
reject archives that require copying or reading beyond the decoder window
keep filter execution state synchronized with window-size updates
use checked arithmetic for seek offsets and compressed-data extents
reject entries whose derived seek positions wrap or move backward unexpectedly
keep reader state transitions single-owner and monotonic across seek calculations

rows.targeted_area: GNU longlink truncation handling in the TAR reader
required_controls: treat truncated GNU longlink payloads as malformed headers
require complete name termination before longlink material is consumed
keep path-buffer length bookkeeping synchronized with truncated input
validate every delta-filter cursor move against the destination window
reject filters whose computed `src` position runs past `dst`
keep filter-state transitions explicit and bounds-checked
validate audio-filter cursor updates before each read or write
forbid filter state that allows `src` to advance beyond `dst`
convert inconsistent filter metadata into a hard decode error

rows.targeted_area: ZIP empty-name handling with mac-ext enabled
required_controls: reject or explicitly special-case empty ZIP entry names before mac-ext processing
keep name-length checks consistent between local and central directory parsing
avoid deriving path or metadata state from zero-length names without an explicit rule

rows.targeted_area: write-to-disk path handling and permission application
required_controls: avoid temporary process-wide `umask()` changes during extraction
create directories with explicit modes and repair permissions without global side effects
document or enforce thread-safety expectations for disk-write APIs

rows.targeted_area: link resolution while applying metadata during extraction
required_controls: apply metadata to opened file descriptors instead of re-resolving paths
re-check containment after filesystem mutations
refuse symlink and hardlink targets that escape the extraction root
treat ACL application as a privileged metadata operation requiring containment checks
avoid following links when setting ACLs
bind ACL updates to the extracted object rather than a later pathname lookup
enforce minimum nonzero header-size constraints before header dispatch
reject corrupted headers before any derived cursor movement or allocation
require header length fields to fit the remaining input window
track visited continuation locations or bound the chain length
guarantee progress on every Rock Ridge parsing step
fail closed when a continuation record repeats or regresses

rows.targeted_area: WARC parser record and body handling
required_controls: cap WARC record sizes and output growth
reject implausible body lengths before streaming work begins
budget CPU and disk work for records that expand attacker-controlled content
validate that each 7zip stream attribute appears only in legal combinations
bound EmptyStream-derived indexing against the file count
treat repeated or contradictory header attributes as malformed input
track current line length separately from total read-ahead size
enforce maximum line sizes before extending the lookahead buffer
recompute parser cursors after read-ahead growth instead of assuming prior offsets stay valid
limit recursive filter nesting depth
cap total decompressed output and intermediate expansion
surface hard failure instead of continuing after budget exhaustion
use checked length accounting for ISO9660 path components and directory records
reject filenames whose encoded size exceeds format limits before buffer or record construction
avoid lossy conversions between host lengths and on-disk field widths

rows.targeted_area: sandboxed extraction of hardlink entries with data
required_controls: treat hardlink entries with data as security-sensitive special cases
never allow sandbox escape through link target resolution
perform path containment and overwrite policy checks before any data write

rows.targeted_area: CPIO symlink-size validation in header parsing
required_controls: cap CPIO symlink target sizes before allocating or decoding the link body
reject entries whose declared symlink length exceeds the remaining archive data
treat oversized or truncated symlink metadata as a hard parse error

rows.targeted_area: ISO parser offset and length calculations
required_controls: use checked add, mul, and shift operations for ISO offsets and sizes
reject records whose claimed layout cannot fit within the input
treat arithmetic overflow as a parse error, not a recoverable condition
cap the number of substreams permitted by a single folder or entry
use checked multiplication when sizing per-substream tables
reject metadata whose aggregate decoded structure cannot fit in memory or the input model
use checked arithmetic for skip amounts and block alignment calculations
reject tar entries whose claimed size would wrap or exceed the remaining stream
preserve monotonic reader-position invariants across skip operations
validate shift counts before any bit operation
bound compression parameters to the format's legal range
convert malformed bidder state into an early parse error
use checked conversions between archive timestamps and host time types
reject mtree values outside representable bounds
keep min and max helper logic free of wraparound assumptions

rows.targeted_area: ISO directory traversal surfaced through bsdtar
required_controls: detect self-referential and cyclic directory structures
bound directory walk depth and node revisits
fail closed when parent-child relationships are inconsistent
normalize signed versus unsigned lengths before offset arithmetic
check every cursor advance against the available buffer
reject malformed ZIP extra fields before partial parsing mutates state
reject absolute paths unless an explicit unsafe mode is enabled
canonicalize and enforce extraction-root containment for every entry
separate archive path semantics from host filesystem path joins

rows.targeted_area: ZIP writer signed and unsigned size conversion
required_controls: use checked conversions when writing ZIP field sizes and offsets
reject host-side lengths that cannot be represented in the target ZIP encoding
test writer behavior on both 32-bit and 64-bit platforms
ensure PAX parsing advances the cursor or aborts
treat EOF inside an extended header as a hard parse failure
bound the number of malformed-record recovery attempts

rows.targeted_area: generic skip logic near physical end of archive
required_controls: reject skip requests that extend past known physical EOF
treat repeated zero-byte reads during a skip as fatal
maintain monotonic stream-position invariants in the reader core
